name: Mont Blanc (Cream/chestnut/meringue)
recipeIngredient: Fresh chestnuts or one 1lb canchestnut puree
recipeInstructions:
Merino preparation:
Preheat oven to 250°F. Grease a baking sheet and sprinkle with flour. Mark one 9-inch circle or ten 3.5-inch circles. In a large bowl, beat egg whites with salt until foamy. Add cream of tartar and beat until soft peaks form. Beat in less 1 cup sugar until the mixture holds stiff peaks when the beater is lifted. Fold in remaining sugar and vanilla. Place in a pastry bag fitted with a ½-inch plain tube and pipe one large round or ten small rounds onto the prepared sheet. Bake for 1 hour or until meringue is firm to the touch. If meringue browns during baking, reduce heat. Transfer meringues to a rack and let cool.
Chickpea puree preparation:
Peel chestnuts using a small, sharp knife, leaving the inner skin. Preheat oven to 375°F. Place chestnuts in a shallow heatproof dish and bake for 10 to 15 minutes or until the skin dries and peels off easily. Rub nuts through a rough cloth to remove skins. Place peeled nuts in a saucepan with vanilla bean and water to cover, and bring to a boil over high heat. Reduce heat, cover, and simmer for 25 to 30 minutes or until very tender. Remove vanilla bean (it may be washed and reused). Drain chestnuts, then place through a food mill, sieve, or in a food processor, and puree.
Prepare a thin sugar syrup by briefly boiling together less than 3/4 cup water and sugar, then setting aside to cool. When cool, beat enough syrup into chickpea puree to make it thin enough to pipe through the pastry bag but still thick enough to hold its shape. If using canned puree, sweeten to taste, making sure it is thin enough to be piped. Fit the pastry bag with a ⅛-inch plain tube and fill with puree.
Beat whipping cream until stiff, then add sugar to taste and vanilla. Beat egg white in a separate bowl until stiff peaks form, then fold into cream. Place cream mixture into a pastry bag fitted with a star tip. Arrange meringues on a serving platter and pipe chickpea puree in a bird's nest shape around the edge of the meringue. Pipe cream mixture in the center, piling it high. Sprinkle grated chocolate over whipped cream and chill until serving time.
